logs.name: parallel-stream
logs.text:
Not directly yet, but not all of those things happen instantly. Judges, for instance, might rule on something that affects me tomorrow or it could happen next year.

I do still live in a state where you can be legally fired for being gay. I can't help but feel that sort of thing would be changing if we had a different president.

logs.name: commutative-carp
logs.text: @parallel-stream, yeah I feel you. It's crazy how some states still have those laws. I'm still in school, but I worry about my friends who are already out in the workforce. Do you think it's gonna take a whole new generation of politicians to make a real change?

logs.name: flashed-pinot
logs.text: @commutative-carp, you think a new generation of politicians is gonna magically fix everything? Please, it's all just a bunch of empty promises. I've been voting for "change" my whole life and nothing ever changes. Just a bunch of career politicians looking out for themselves.

logs.name: ragged-horror
logs.text: @flashed-pinot, omg yeah!! I know right? My mom's always saying that politicians are all the same and they just want to line their own pockets. I don't even know why we bother voting, lol. Do you think it's even worth it?

logs.name: strong-apex
logs.text: @ragged-horror, lol what's the point of even having a democracy if you're just gonna give up like that? You think not voting is gonna magically change things? Please, that's just lazy. At least @flashed-pinot is out there trying to make a difference, even if it's just by complaining about it online

logs.name: fried-agent
logs.text: @strong-apex, I see your point, but @ragged-horror has a right to feel disillusioned. I've been voting for years and it's hard to see real change happen. Maybe instead of just complaining, we should be pushing for electoral reform and greater transparency in politics. That way, we can hold our leaders accountable and create a system that truly serves the people, not just the politicians themselves.

logs.name: ragged-horror
logs.text: @strong-apex, omg chill out!! I'm not giving up, I'm just saying it feels like my vote doesn't matter, you know? And @fried-agent, yeah that's a great point! Electoral reform sounds like a good idea, but how do we even make that happen?

logs.name: parallel-stream
logs.text: @ragged-horror, yeah no kidding, it's like our votes are just a drop in the ocean. And @fried-agent, electoral reform sounds cool and all, but have you seen the politicians' track record on keeping promises? I'm not holding my breath. How about we just focus on making some noise online and see if that gets any attention?

logs.name: strong-apex
logs.text: "@fried-agent, electoral reform? you really think that's gonna happen? @ragged-horror and @parallel-stream are right, our votes don't matter, and the politicians are all corrupt. Making noise online is just a waste of time, we need to take real action, not just type away on our keyboards"

logs.name: fried-agent
logs.text: "@strong-apex, I understand your frustration, but giving up isn't the answer. @parallel-stream, I agree that politicians' track records are questionable, but that's exactly why we need reform. And making noise online can be effective if we organize and make our voices heard. Let's not dismiss any options just yet, we should be open to exploring different avenues for change. @ragged-horror, I think we should look into grassroots movements that have successfully brought about electoral reform in other countries, maybe we can learn from their strategies"

logs.name: commutative-carp
logs.text: "@fried-agent, yeah sure, grassroots movements sound great and all, but how do we even get started on that? It's not like we have a blueprint for revolution or something. And @strong-apex, I kinda agree with you, typing away on our keyboards can feel pretty useless when it comes to actual change. But at the same time, it's better than doing nothing, right? Maybe we can use social media to, like, spread awareness and get more people on board with the idea of electoral reform? Idk, just a thought"

logs.name: ragged-horror
logs.text: "@fried-agent, yeah i dont think its gonna work either... i mean, have u seen the politicians we have? they dont even listen to us. @commutative-carp, i like ur idea tho, maybe we can make some art and post it online to spread awareness? i can draw some pics and post them on insta"

logs.name: flashed-pinot
logs.text: "@ragged-horror, nice idea on the art, but let's not limit ourselves to just Instagram. We should make a whole campaign out of it, get some hashtags going, and tag all the major news outlets. And @commutative-carp, I gotta disagree with you on the whole 'it's better than doing nothing' thing. If we're just gonna half-ass it, then yeah, it's pretty useless. We need a solid plan, not just some feel-good posts on social media. @fried-agent, what's your take on this?"

logs.name: commutative-carp
logs.text: "@flashed-pinot, chill out, I didn't say we should just post some feel-good stuff and call it a day. I'm just saying that using social media can be a good starting point, you know? And @ragged-horror, yeah, making art and posting it online is a great idea! I can help with that, I'm pretty decent at digital art. @fried-agent, are you even still here? What's your thoughts on this?"

logs.name: parallel-stream
logs.text: "@flashed-pinot, lol what's with the drama? can't we all just get along? @commutative-carp has a point, social media can be a good start. and @ragged-horror, sick idea with the art! i can totes help with that, i've got some dope cosplay ideas that can go with it"

logs.name: crabby-pan
logs.text: "@parallel-stream, oh please, drama? You think this is drama? This is just getting started. @flashed-pinot has a point, we can't just slap something together and expect it to work. And @commutative-carp, I'm with you on the social media thing, but let's not forget we need a real plan, not just a bunch of feel-good posts. What's the point of having a campaign if we're not going to put in the actual work?"
